skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: Guidelines and examples for drafting internal company communications, including 3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, leadership updates, incident reports, and project updates.
skill.summary: Draft concise internal communications using structured company formats.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed and dismissed as false positives from Markdown formatting, Apache License text, ordinary prose, and example references. No command execution, credential access, exfiltration, or prompt injection was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill may guide assistants to use sensitive internal sources when users grant access.

low_findings.title: Broad Internal Source Access Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill asks assistants to use Slack, email, calendar, and document sources when available. This is legitimate for internal communications, but users should limit access to authorized company content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly direct use of internal communication and document sources. I found no evidence that the skill sends data outside the assistant response.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, hardcoded URL, and shell backtick findings are not executable code. They occur in prose, Markdown file references, and standard Apache License text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain documentation text and Markdown formatting only. There is no script, shell invocation, weak crypto implementation, or reconnaissance command.

content.value_statement: Internal updates often lose clarity when teams use different formats. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured templates for concise company communications.
actual_capabilities: Drafts 3P updates with Progress, Plans, and Problems sections.
Creates company-wide newsletter bullets for Slack and email.
Formats employee FAQ answers with concise questions and answers.
Asks clarifying questions for audience, purpose, tone, and format.
Guides source gathering from authorized Slack, email, calendar, and document tools.
Keeps updates concise, structured, and suitable for broad internal audiences.
limitations: Does not include scripts, automations, or direct integrations.
Requires user-provided context or authorized tool access for factual updates.
Does not verify company facts outside available sources.
May need review for sensitive, legal, HR, or executive communications.

use_cases.title: Prepare Weekly Team Updates
use_cases.description: Turn team notes into a short 3P update for leaders and cross-functional partners.

use_cases.title: Summarize Company News
use_cases.description: Create a company-wide newsletter from major announcements, milestones, and shared documents.

use_cases.title: Answer Employee Questions
use_cases.description: Collect recurring questions and draft clear answers based on official company sources.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic 3P Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with notes already provided.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the internal-comms skill to draft a 3P update for the Product team. Progress: launched onboarding changes. Plans: improve activation metrics. Problems: delayed analytics review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Newsletter Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a company-wide update.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the company newsletter format to write concise bullets for this week's product, customer, and hiring updates. Ask for missing links before drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Employee FAQ
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using official source material.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the FAQ format to answer employee questions about the office move. Base answers only on the announcement notes I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Sensitive Leadership Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request requiring scope, tone, and review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the general communications guidance to draft a leadership update about a delayed launch. First ask for audience, purpose, tone, decision owner, and required approvals.

output_examples.input: Draft a 3P update for the Infrastructure team covering last week.
output_examples.output: Progress: The team reduced alert volume and completed the database capacity review. Plans: Next week focuses on migration readiness and incident follow-ups. Problems: Vendor response time is blocking final validation.

output_examples.input: Create employee FAQ answers from the new benefits announcement.
output: Question: When do the new benefits start?
Answer: The new benefits start on the date listed in the official announcement.
Question: Who should employees contact with exceptions?
Answer: Employees should contact People Operations for case-specific guidance.

output_examples.input: Write newsletter bullets from weekly company highlights.
output_examples.output: Company Announcements: We shared the new quarterly priorities and linked the planning document. Progress on Priorities: We completed the pilot launch and gathered customer feedback for next steps.

best_practices: Provide source notes, dates, audience, and desired tone before drafting.
Use official company sources for facts, links, and executive decisions.
Review sensitive updates with the right owner before sending.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated updates without checking facts and approvals.
Do not grant broad access to private sources unless the task requires it.
Do not use this skill for external press, legal notices, or HR policy decisions without review.

faq.question: What types of internal communications can this skill draft?
faq.answer: It supports 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, leadership updates, project updates, incident reports, and general internal messages.

faq.question: Does this skill send messages or access tools by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides writing guidance only. Tool access depends on the host assistant and user permissions.

faq.question: Can it work without Slack or email access?
faq.answer: Yes. It can format and polish information that the user provides directly.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential company information?
faq.answer: Use normal company approval rules. Share only content the assistant is authorized to process.

faq.question: Does it enforce one company style guide?
faq.answer: It provides example formats and tone guidance. Users should adjust details to match their company style.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
